name: Moussaka
instructions: Slice and fry the eggplants and potatoes. Prepare the minced meat sauce with onions and tomatoes. Make the béchamel sauce. Layer potatoes, eggplants, minced meat, and béchamel in a baking pan, and bake at 180°C until golden brown.

name: Pastitsio
instructions: Boil the pasta. Cook the minced meat sauce with onion, tomato, and allspice. Prepare the béchamel sauce. Layer half the pasta, the minced meat, the remaining pasta, and top with béchamel. Bake until golden.

name: Tzatziki
instructions: Grate the cucumber and squeeze it tightly to remove all excess moisture. Mix the Greek yogurt with minced garlic, olive oil, vinegar, dill, and the strained cucumber.

name: Gemista (Stuffed Vegetables)
instructions: Hollow out the tomatoes and bell peppers. Mix the scooped tomato flesh with rice, finely chopped onion, parsley, and olive oil. Stuff the vegetables, place potato wedges in between, and bake.

name: Fasolada (Greek Bean Soup)
instructions: Soak the white beans overnight. Boil them, discard the first water, then boil again with chopped onion, carrots, celery, tomato paste, and olive oil until thick and creamy.

name: Spanakopita (Spinach Pie)
instructions: Sauté the spinach and green onions. Mix with crumbled feta cheese, eggs, and dill. Layer half the phyllo sheets, greasing each one, add the filling, cover with remaining sheets, and bake.

name: Lahanodolmades (Stuffed Cabbage Rolls)
instructions: Blanch the cabbage leaves. Mix the minced meat with rice, finely chopped onion, and dill. Roll the mixture into the leaves, place them in a pot, cook, and top with avgolemono (egg-lemon sauce).

name: Kleftiko (Parchment-Baked Lamb)
instructions: Cut lamb and potatoes. Marinate with garlic, lemon juice, and oregano. Wrap them tightly in parchment paper with cubes of kefalotyri cheese and bell peppers, and slow-bake.

name: Giouvetsi
instructions: Brown the beef with onion and tomato sauce. Once tender, transfer to a clay pot, add hot water and orzo pasta, and bake in the oven until the pasta cooks.

name: Fakes (Greek Lentil Soup)
instructions: Boil lentils for 5 minutes and strain. Bring to a boil again with fresh water, chopped onion, garlic, bay leaves, and tomato paste. Stir in olive oil and vinegar at the end.

name: Bakaliaros Skordalia (Cod with Garlic Dip)
instructions: De-salt the cod. Make a batter with flour and beer, dip the cod pieces, and deep fry. Make the skordalia by mashing boiled potatoes with garlic, olive oil, and vinegar.

name: Keftedakia (Greek Meatballs)
instructions: Knead the minced meat with soaked bread, grated onion, fresh mint, and egg. Shape into small balls, roll in flour, and fry in hot oil.

name: Soutzoukakia Smyrneika
instructions: Knead minced beef with garlic, cumin, and bread. Shape into oblong meatballs, lightly fry them, and then simmer in a rich tomato sauce.

name: Roasted Chicken and Potatoes
instructions: Cut potatoes and chicken. Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, oregano, and garlic in a bowl. Pour over the chicken and potatoes in a pan and bake at 200°C.

name: Gigantes Plaki (Baked Giant Beans)
instructions: Boil the giant beans until slightly tender. Transfer to a baking pan with a sauce made of tomato, onion, garlic, parsley, and plenty of olive oil, and bake.

name: Melitzanosalata (Eggplant Dip)
instructions: Roast whole eggplants until charred. Scoop out the flesh, chop finely, and mix with minced garlic, olive oil, vinegar, and fresh parsley.

name: Pizza Margherita
instructions: Roll out the dough, spread the tomato sauce, top with fresh mozzarella pieces, and bake at a very high temperature. Garnish with fresh basil leaves.

name: Spaghetti Carbonara
instructions: Boil the spaghetti. Sauté the guanciale/pancetta until crispy. Whisk egg yolks with Pecorino Romano and black pepper. Toss the hot pasta with meat and egg mixture off the heat.

name: Lasagna Bolognese
instructions: Prepare the meat ragù by slow-cooking minced meat with onions, carrots, celery, and tomato sauce. Layer lasagna sheets, ragù, béchamel, and parmesan cheese in a pan. Bake until bubbling.

name: Mushroom Risotto
instructions: Sauté onion and mushrooms. Add Arborio rice, toast lightly, then deglaze with white wine. Gradually add hot vegetable broth, stirring constantly. Stir in butter and parmesan at the end.

name: Tiramisu
instructions: Whisk egg yolks with sugar and fold in mascarpone. Dip ladyfingers briefly in espresso and layer them. Spread the cream mixture, repeat layers, and dust with cocoa powder.

name: Spaghetti with Pesto
instructions: Blend or pound basil leaves, garlic, pine nuts, parmesan, pecorino, and olive oil into a smooth paste. Toss with freshly boiled spaghetti.

name: Potato Gnocchi
instructions: Boil potatoes and mash them. Mix with flour and egg to form a soft dough. Shape into small pieces, mark with a fork, and boil until they float to the surface.

name: Tomato Bruschetta
instructions: Toast rustic bread slices and rub them with a garlic clove. Dice tomatoes, toss with basil and olive oil, and spoon over the bread.

name: Chicken Cacciatore
instructions: Sear chicken pieces. Sauté onion and carrot, deglaze with red wine. Add crushed tomatoes and rosemary, and simmer until the sauce thickens.

name: Panna Cotta
instructions: Heat heavy cream with sugar and vanilla. Stir in soaked gelatin sheets until dissolved, pour into molds, and refrigerate until set. Serve with strawberry syrup.

name: Ratatouille
instructions: Slice zucchini, eggplants, and tomatoes into thin rounds. Spread tomato sauce at the bottom of a baking dish, arrange the slices alternately in a circular pattern, drizzle with olive oil and thyme, and bake.

name: Coq au Vin
instructions: Marinate chicken in red wine. Sauté bacon, mushrooms, and shallots. Sear the chicken, pour in the wine marinade, and simmer slowly for 2 hours.

name: French Onion Soup
instructions: Slow-cook onions in butter for a long time until perfectly caramelized. Deglaze with white wine, add beef broth, and simmer. Serve topped with a slice of baguette and melted Gruyere cheese.

name: Beef Bourguignon
instructions: Sear beef chunks. Add carrots and onions, pour in red wine and beef broth, toss in a bouquet garni, and simmer on low heat for 3 hours until beef is melt-in-your-mouth tender.

name: Crème Brûlée
instructions: Heat cream with vanilla. Whisk egg yolks with sugar, incorporate the warm cream, and bake in a bain-marie. Chill, sprinkle with brown sugar, and caramelize with a blowtorch.

name: Paella Valenciana
instructions: Brown the chicken and rabbit in the paella pan. Add green beans and grated tomato. Pour in hot stock with saffron dissolved in it, then add the rice. Cook on medium heat without stirring until the liquid is absorbed and a crust (socarrat) forms at the bottom.

name: Spanish Potato Tortilla
instructions: Slice potatoes and onion thinly and cook gently in plenty of olive oil until soft. Drain, mix with beaten eggs, season with salt. Cook the omelette in a lightly oiled pan on both sides, flipping carefully with a plate.

name: Gazpacho Soup
instructions: Soak the bread briefly in water and squeeze dry. Blend all vegetables with the bread, olive oil, and sherry vinegar until completely smooth. Season with salt, chill for at least 2 hours, and serve ice-cold.

name: Churros with Chocolate
instructions: Bring water and butter to a boil, add flour and stir vigorously until a smooth dough forms. Beat in eggs one at a time. Pipe through a star nozzle into hot oil and fry until golden. Roll in sugar and cinnamon and serve with melted dark chocolate.

name: Garlic Shrimp (Gambas al Ajillo)
instructions: Heat olive oil and gently saute the garlic and chilli. Add shrimp, cook 2 min, deglaze with white wine, and sprinkle with fresh parsley. Serve immediately, sizzling.

name: Kung Pao Chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken in soy sauce and cornstarch. Stir-fry garlic, ginger, and dried chillies in a very hot wok. Add chicken, diced pepper, and extra soy sauce. Finish with roasted peanuts.

name: Chinese Fried Rice
instructions: Stir-fry carrots and peas in a hot wok. Push to the side and scramble the eggs. Add cold cooked rice, stir in soy sauce, spring onions, and a drizzle of sesame oil over high heat.

name: Sweet and Sour Pork
instructions: Coat pork in cornstarch and deep-fry until golden. Make the sauce with ketchup, vinegar, sugar, and a splash of water. Add pineapple, peppers, and onion, bring to a simmer, then toss in the fried pork.

name: Chinese Dumplings
instructions: Mix pork with cabbage, spring onions, ginger, and soy sauce. Place filling on each wrapper, fold into a half-moon and crimp edges. Steam for 8 min or pan-fry until the base is golden, then add a splash of water and cover to steam through.

name: Spring Rolls
instructions: Stir-fry vegetables with ginger and soy sauce until just tender. Allow to cool. Place filling on each wrapper, roll tightly tucking in the sides. Deep-fry in hot oil until crispy and golden.

name: Bibimbap
instructions: Cook each vegetable separately with sesame oil and soy sauce. Saute the marinated beef. Place rice in a bowl, arrange vegetables and meat in sections, add a fried egg in the centre, and serve with gochujang paste.

name: Kimchi Stew (Kimchi Jjigae)
instructions: Saute pork belly and kimchi together in a pot. Add 500ml stock, garlic, and gochugaru, simmer 15 min. Add tofu and spring onions, cook 5 more min. Serve bubbling hot with steamed rice.

name: Beef Bulgogi
instructions: Marinate beef in soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, garlic, spring onions, and grated pear for at least 2 hours. Cook in a very hot pan or grill for 1-2 min per side until caramelised.

name: Tteokbokki (Spicy Rice Cakes)
instructions: Bring dashi stock to a simmer, dissolve gochujang and sugar. Add rice cakes and fish cakes, simmer 10-15 min until sauce thickens and rice cakes are soft and chewy. Garnish with spring onions.

name: Japchae (Stir-Fried Glass Noodles)
instructions: Soak noodles in hot water 20 min then boil briefly and drain. Saute each vegetable and the beef separately. Combine everything with noodles, soy sauce, sesame oil, and a pinch of sugar. Toss well and serve warm.

name: Butter Chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt and garam masala for 2 hours, then grill or bake until slightly charred. Saute garlic and ginger in butter, add crushed tomatoes and simmer 15 min. Blend sauce smooth, stir in cream, add chicken and simmer 10 min.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt with cumin, coriander, and turmeric for 2 hours, then grill until slightly charred. Prepare spiced tomato sauce with onion, ginger, garlic, and cream. Add chicken and simmer 10 min. Serve with basmati rice or naan.

name: Chicken Biryani
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices. Cook chicken in a heavy pot. Par-boil basmati rice with whole spices until 70% done. Layer rice and chicken alternately, drizzle saffron milk on top, seal lid, and slow-cook on very low heat 30 min (Dum method).

name: Chana Masala (Chickpea Curry)
instructions: Saute onion, ginger, and garlic until golden. Add tomatoes, turmeric, and garam masala, cook until oil separates. Add chickpeas with a splash of water and simmer 15 min until sauce is thick. Finish with lemon juice.

name: Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach then blend to a smooth puree. Saute onion, ginger, garlic, and garam masala until fragrant. Add spinach puree and cream, simmer 5 min. Add pan-fried paneer cubes and heat through. Serve with naan or basmati rice.

name: Roasted Lamb with Potatoes
instructions: Cut potatoes and make small incisions in the lamb to insert garlic. Mix olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, and oregano, and pour over everything. Bake covered at 180°C for 2.5 hours.

name: Shrimp Saganaki
instructions: Sauté onion, garlic, and pepper. Add shrimp, flambé with ouzo, and pour in the crushed tomatoes. Simmer for 10 minutes, add crumbled feta cheese, and let it melt.

name: Okra in Olive Oil
instructions: Drizzle okra with vinegar and dry them. Sauté onion and garlic, add okra, ripe tomatoes, and olive oil, then simmer on low heat without stirring heavily.

name: Sifnos Chickpea Stew
instructions: Soak chickpeas with baking soda, then rinse thoroughly. Place in a traditional clay pot with lots of chopped onions, olive oil, water, and bay leaves. Seal the lid with dough and slow-bake for 6-8 hours.
